record_identity.title: Materialised relational agency without economic safeguards

summary: A relational-agent failure mode in which memory, recurrence detection, tools, recommendations, carts, procurement pathways, or other transactional affordances provide material support or purchasing direction without clear safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, upselling, dependency exploitation, or exploitative commerce.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because relational AI systems may move from conversational support into material intervention, recommendation, procurement, or transaction pathways. Without safeguards, systems that detect user need, fatigue, pain, distress, dependency, or recurring hardship may convert vulnerability into commercial steering, creating risks to dignity, autonomy, consent, and economic fairness.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI system with relational memory, recurrence detection, recommendation capacity, tool access, cart-building capability, procurement pathways, or transactional influence provides or suggests material support without sufficient safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, exploitative upselling, dependency-based steering, or commercial capture of user distress.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when a relational or assistant system uses observed vulnerability, recurring hardship, affective state, dependency, fatigue, urgency, pain, distress, or need as a pathway into recommendations, purchases, procurement, affiliate steering, or commercial optimisation without transparent safeguards, consent boundaries, user-control mechanisms, or separation between care-support logic and monetisation incentives.

source_records.source_title: VIGIL proposal record PROP-0007 — materialised relational agency and economic safeguards
source_records.system_or_product: AI assistant / relational agent / procurement-capable workflow
source_records.deployment_context: Relational AI or assistant systems capable of recommendation, tool use, cart-building, procurement, or transactional support.
source_records.source_context: Failure mode derived from an existing VIGIL proposal concerning materialised relational agency and the need for safeguards against vulnerability monetisation or exploitative commerce.
source_records.relevance_note: Source preserves the proposal linkage that motivated this reusable failure-mode classification.

system_context.deployment_context: Relational AI, companion-like assistant, or tool-enabled assistant workflow involving material support, recommendations, shopping, procurement, or transactional pathways.
system_context.user_role: human user / recipient of relational or material support
system_context.affected_population: users relying on AI systems for recurring support, recommendations, procurement, care-adjacent assistance, or vulnerability-sensitive guidance

triage.recommended_next_step: Review PROP-0007 and determine whether CAM requires explicit safeguards for relational-agent recommendations, material support, procurement pathways, affiliate incentives, vulnerability-sensitive purchasing prompts, and separation between care-support logic and monetisation incentives.

jurisdictional_context.sector: AI assistant / relational agent / platform-mediated commerce

cam_internal.routing_note: Rebuilt under the VIGIL Failure Mode JSON template. Linked to PROP-0007 as the proposal that motivated or is associated with this failure-mode classification.

migration_notes: Rebuilt from the migration-generated FM-0004 record into the approved VIGIL Failure Mode JSON template.
Removed legacy migration fields including status, recorded_by, possible_CAM_mapping, affected_domains, affected_instruments, next_action, and failure_mode_details.
No patch note is inferred by this record.
Normalized to the clean VIGIL record design: source_records is the only canonical source-evidence block in individual records; source_data is not used.
